my_thoughts: The denial works: chief feature = weakness (not strength), we have no strength. The student is resisting negative self-concept (ego defense), which shows they're trying to protect self-image. They'll likely pose logical paradox next.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student asks moral question ('Must the chief feature necessarily be bad?')—resisting negative self-concept via asks for moral judgment and seeks rehabilitation. They're trying to reframe weakness as strength (ego defense). The constraint_collapse mechanism denies: chief feature = weakness (not strength), we have no strength ('unfortunately we cannot think that our chief feature is strength, because we have no strength'). Pattern: criterion inquiry → structural model → moral question → denial → [next: logical paradox]. They're resisting: weakness = bad (moral judgment).
